title: tarigidia aequiglumis
intro: Tarigidia aequiglumis, also known as the Native Violet, is a plant species native to Australia, characterized by its small, purple flowers and heart-shaped leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Tarigidia aequiglumis include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, and is also used topically as a poultice or cream to apply directly to the skin.
